items.title: Infinia, you served me well. ❤️ You funded flights for my family for two Europe trips, several South East Asia trips, and countless domestic flights over the years. ✈️🌍 And even today, you’ve left me with a stash of points that is enough for one more family trip to Europe (or any other far-off destination). 😊 But it seems you have lived your life, and the time has come for you to retire. 🫡 The newer players are now offering better rewards, a better overall experience, and look far more promising. 🚀 I still hope you make a strong comeback someday. Until then, it’s time to hand over the baton to the new players. 👋 Loyalty? ❓😏 There are better things to be loyal to. I keep credit cards for the benefits they offer. Whichever card gives me the best value based on my spending patterns earns a place in my wallet. 💳 At the end of the day, a credit card is just a financial tool—not an emotion. Use it while it serves you well, and don’t hesitate to move on when something better comes along. 😊. #creditcard #spendwisely #creditcard

items.title: 💳 An important lesson for all credit card users to learn ! ⚠️ Unlike UPI or Net Banking, where money is transferred to the beneficiary almost instantly, credit card transactions are not settled immediately. In most cases, a credit card transaction takes 1–2 days to settle. This characteristic has both advantages and disadvantages. ✅ The Advantage: Since the transaction takes time to settle and reach the beneficiary’s account, if you notice any suspicious or fraudulent transaction, you can immediately report it to the card issuer. In many cases, the transaction can be put on hold or even reversed before settlement. This is not possible with most UPI transactions as the money is transferred instantly in UPI. ⚠️ The Disadvantage: If you’re trying to meet the spend deadline for a credit card offer, milestone benefit, welcome bonus, or any other benefit calculated on a calendar month basis, many card issuers consider the settlement date to determine eligibility, not the actual transaction date. And because if this, you may miss the deadline. 📅 So, wherever possible, don’t wait until the deadline to complete your spending. Make the transaction a couple of days in advance to avoid missing out on valuable benefits. ❓Have you ever missed a credit card offer or milestone because you transacted on the last day, but the issuer considered the settlement date and denied the benefit? 💬 Share your experience in the comments! 👇 ⸻ 📘 Visit SpendWisely Blog: Detailed credit card reviews & guides 👉 https://spendwisely.in Some links may be referral links in the above post.
